sutta:
the buddha said monks i will teach the 7 fairings of man and the completely unattached cool listen well pay heed i will speak yes lord rejoined the monks and the exalted 1 said and what monks are the 7 fairings of man consider a monk who steps his way thinking if it were not he would not be mine it shall not become for me it shall not become what is what's become that i abandoned he acquires equanimity he earns not after life he earns not after life to come but sees with true wisdom that there is beyond a goal a peace yet not all in all is the goal realized by him not all in all is the leaning towards conceit got rid of not that towards worldly lust nor towards ignorance when the 5 lower factors are destroyed it becomes completely cool after an interval monks just as from an iron slab heated and beaten all day a bit may come off and cool down even so a monk who steps his way thinking thus acquires equanimity and when the 5 lower factors are destroyed becomes completely cool after an interval again consider a monk who thinking likewise becomes completely cool after an interval just as from an iron slab beaten and heated and beaten all day a bit may come off fly up and cool down even so a monk becomes completely cool after an interval all the bit may come off fly up and before touching the ground cool down even so a monk becomes completely cool after an interval consider a monk who thinking likewise becomes completely cool after lessening his period just as the bit may come off fly up and after touching the ground cool down even so a monk becomes cool after lessening his period consider the monk who without exertion becomes completely cool just as the bit may come off fly up and fall on some small heap of grass heap of sticks kindle fire kindle smoke and consume that small heap and cool down from want of fuel even so among without exertion becomes completely cool consider the monk who with some exertion becomes completely cool just as the bit may fall on a large heap of grass heap of sticks kindle fire kindle smoke and consume that large heap and cool down from 1 to fewer even so among with some exertion becomes completely cool consider a man who thinks likewise acquires equanimity earns not after life or life to come but sees the goal yet not all in all realizes it not all in all has got rid of the leanings towards conceit only lust and ignorance but when the 5 lower factors are destroyed it becomes part of the upward stream bound for the highest just as from an iron slab heated and beaten all day a bit may come off fly up and fall on a large heap of grass a large heap of sticks kindle fire kindle smoke consumed that large heap set fire to the shrub land set fire to the woodland and burning there come to the edge of a green cornfield upland rock water or lush countryside and there cool down from want or fuel even so a monk who steps his way thinking thus on destroying the 5 lower fatters becomes part of the upward stream bound for the highest for really monks these are the 7 fairings of man and what is a completely unattached cool consider a man who steps his way thinking if it were not mine if it were not it would not be mine it shall not become for me it shall not become what is what's become that i abandoned he acquires equanimity he earns not after life he ears not after life to come but with true wisdom sees that there is beyond a goal a peace and that goal is verily all in all realized by him and all in all is the leaning towards conceit god read off by him the leaning towards worldly lust and leaning towards ignorance destroying the asavas he enters and abides in the asava free emancipation by mind emancipation by wisdom here and now realizing it himself by his own knowledge this month is called a completely unattached cool verily monks these are the 7 pharynx of men and the completely unattached cool this uttar is 1 of those which are a bit difficult firstly the buddha talks about this passage if it were not it would not be mine and the commentary says what it means it is if it were not for my past karma my present body person would not exist i repeat that if it were not for my past karma my present body person would not exist and then it continues it shall not become for me it shall not become was what is what's become that i abandoned in other words if i don't create any more kama then it shall not i shall not have this body again and what is the present body that i abandoned um but if you think about it instead of karma we could also say craving if you are not for craving i would not be i would not exist today or if it were not if it were not for ignorance in the past i would not exist today and if i could cut off my craving or ignorance now then i shall not become again and what is that means the present grieving or the present ignorance that i abandoned so this can equally apply apply to craving and to ignorance now this after that now this tutor talks about the symbolism for the 5 types of anagamin a non-returner the third fruition person and this is the only suta which gives this similar for the 5 types of the first type is called an antara parinibadi this is a person the undergarmin who becomes completely cool after an interval and the simile given is that a blacksmith heats up an iron slab the whole day and he uh beat on this iron slab he heats it up and he beats it now the whole day and a chipmunk a bit of the iron slap now achievement flies over and then it cools down holds down before the touching the ground so fast it cools down this is the antara parinibai the first type of anagamin who becomes completely cool after a short interval the second type is called the upahacha parinibai this is the 1 who becomes cool after lessening his period and the simile given here is that the iron slab that is heated and beaten a and after touching the ground as soon as it touches the ground it cools down so this is the second type of phantogamine the third type is called the ah sankara parinibai and this is the 1 who without exertion he becomes completely cool enters nibana and the simile given here is that that bitner from the iron slagman which is heated and beaten the whole day that bit flies off and falls on a small heap of grass sticks a small heap of grass and sticks and makes the small heap of grass and sticks catch fire and kindles fire and kindle smoke and then that small hema is burnt and this iron beta cools down now that's the third time the fourth type now is called the samsung kara parinibai this is the anagamin who with some exertion becomes completely cool and the simile given here is that that bit from that heated iron slab falls on a large heap of grass and sticks and it kindles fire and kindle smoke and consumes the large heap of sticks and grass before it cools down and then the the last time of anagamin is called the udang sota and this is called the anagamin who becomes part of the upward stream bound for the highest and this 1 takes the longest and the simile given here is the pit flies off and falls on a large heap of grass and sticks and then it kindles fire and kindle smokes and it consumes that large heap and in addition it sets fire to the neighboring shrubland sets fire to the woodland and keeps on burning until it comes to the fire comes to the edge of a green field or comes to rock or water and then it stops so this refers to the type of who passes through the 5 levels of heavens in the sudabasa abodes there are 5 levels of heavens so he goes from 1 level to another and finally enters nirvana from this similarly we can see that an anagamin in a way has finished his work on the worldly plane she says is iron bitna from the the the bit from the iron slab which has been heated and beaten the whole day the whole day when it flies up and falls down it must cool down after some time without it uh it must cool down naturally so in the same way all other garments whether they take a short time or they take a longer time in the sudabasa boats and the fourth jhana eventually they cool down even though they don't make any effort they still cool down to nibana
